response.justification: Szabo has the right seniority level and is currently working with MCP and agentic frameworks at re:cinq, showing active engagement with the required technology stack. However, the resume reveals critical gaps: no Python experience across a 13+ year career and zero demonstrated background in building AI-driven solutions, despite the role requiring 8+ years in this domain. Without evidence of RAG systems, vector databases, LLM optimization, or multi-agent frameworks, it's unclear if he possesses the AI/ML domain expertise needed for this staff-level position, though his recent hire at an AI-focused company and strong fundamentals warrant a conversation.
strengths: Currently Staff Software Engineer at re:cinq working directly with MCP and agentic frameworks, demonstrating active engagement with the AI technology stack required for this role
Proven staff-level technical leadership and mentoring across distributed teams, with strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pair programming) that support engineering excellence
Extensive TypeScript expertise and cloud experience (AWS CDK, microservices, high-performance systems) with demonstrated ability to architect scalable backend infrastructure
concerns: No Python programming mentioned across 13+ years of professional experience, despite Python being the primary language for AI/ML engineering
Resume lacks the required 8+ years building and shipping production-grade AI solutions—career shows full-stack and DevOps focus rather than AI/ML systems development
No demonstrated experience with core AI technologies: vector databases, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) architectures, LLM optimization, or multi-agent system frameworks

key_factors.factor: Missing Python despite senior engineering career
No documented AI/ML domain background (required 8+ years)
Current MCP/agentic role + staff seniority + strong TypeScript
